dict.string: Used to open Terminal so you can run `claude /login` when adding an account.
Copyright © 2026 Thanh Ngô. Licensed under MIT.
Claude Bar 11.19 — menu-bar manager for multiple Claude Code accounts

Auto-swap now treats any live claude session — including idle interactive shells and backgrounded `claude -p` script runs — as a hard block. The previous "busy/waiting" check only paused swaps mid-generation, so an idle-but-open claude window would still get its account binding flipped underneath it and force a restart on next prompt. The new safety gate counts every session with Kind=interactive or Kind=bg; daemons and MCP/SDK workers are still ignored so persistent background services don't deadlock the swap loop. When the active account first crosses the threshold, the state machine now arms a 60-second grace deadline instead of attempting an immediate swap. A notification fires once at arming: "Auto-swap in 60s — will switch to <account> if no claude session is running." After the deadline the swap fires only if the safety gate still holds; if a session reappeared, the pending swap waits indefinitely until claude exits. The grace window also auto-cancels if the active percent drops back below threshold (e.g. user manually swaps, or the 5-hour rolling window resets), so we never swap when the trigger has already gone away. Menu-bar safety badge label updated from "claude busy" to "claude running" to match the new semantics — the badge now reflects whether any claude is alive, not just whether one is mid-generation.

Each web-poll cycle still loads a hidden WKWebView reload of claude.ai/settings/usage per linked account and waits for the React SPA to hydrate (typically 1–3s, hard-capped at 12s). At the 120s / 60s cadence this fits comfortably for 2–3 accounts; users with many more web-linked accounts may want to disable web-link on the less-used ones. The reset-clause regex doesn't yet parse every locale's date format (e.g., "Resets Fri 7:00 PM"). When parsing fails the widget falls back to a future placeholder (now + 5h for the session bar, now + 7d for the weekly bar) so the percentage still displays — the countdown will be approximate until the next successful parse. The menu-bar popover still dismisses when the Rename window opens (macOS menu-bar app behaviour, not preventable without giving up the dropdown style). The rename itself goes through on Save; reopen the menu to see the new name. Existing v11.x Google tokens are still missing the `spreadsheets` and `drive.file` scopes shipped for Sheets write + Drive sharing. The cb_gsheets_* and cb_gdrive_share_file tools return Google "insufficient permission" until you Disconnect + Reconnect the Google connector — that mints a fresh token with the new scope set. Drive / Calendar / Gmail read tools keep working on the old scope until you do.
